docs.title: AgentKit for AWS Pulumi
docs.text: Pulumi components for deploying agentkit-based agents to AWS.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Native Go Pulumi components for AWS Bedrock AgentCore deployment
  • AWS Lightsail Container Service deployment provider
  • Fluent builder API matching agentkit-aws-cdk patterns
  • Configuration loading from JSON or YAML files

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi

docs.title: Lightsail Container Deployment
docs.text:
Deploy agents to AWS Lightsail Container Service:

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/agentkit/deploy"
    _ "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi/deploy/providers/lightsail"
)

cfg, _ := deploy.LoadDeployConfig("deploy.yaml")
provider, _ := deploy.GetProvider(cfg)
defer provider.Close()

status, _ := provider.Deploy(ctx, cfg)
fmt.Println(status.Outputs["serviceUrl"])

docs.title: AgentCore with Go Constructs
docs.text:
Type-safe Go code with full IDE support:

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi/agentcore"
    "github.com/pulumi/pulumi/sdk/v3/go/pulumi"
)

func main() {
    pulumi.Run(func(ctx *pulumi.Context) error {
        research := agentcore.NewAgentBuilder("research", "ghcr.io/example/research:latest").
            WithMemory(512).
            Build()

        _, err := agentcore.NewStackBuilder("my-agents").
            WithAgents(research).
            WithNewVPC("10.0.0.0/16", 2).
            Build(ctx)

        return err
    })
}

docs.title: AgentCore with YAML Config
docs.text:
Configuration-driven deployments with minimal code:

func main() {
    pulumi.Run(func(ctx *pulumi.Context) error {
        _, err := agentcore.NewStackFromFile(ctx, "config.yaml")
        return err
    })
}

docs.title: AWS Deployment Guide
docs.text: This guide covers deployment options for agentkit-based agents on AWS.

docs.title: Deployment Targets
docs.text: Target Type Best For Status Bedrock AgentCore Serverless microVMs Pay-per-invoke, zero ops Preview EKS Kubernetes Long-running, high control GA ECS/Fargate Serverless containers Serverless with container flexibility GA

docs.title: Bedrock AgentCore
docs.text: AWS Bedrock AgentCore runs agents in Firecracker microVMs with automatic scaling and pay-per-invocation pricing.

docs.title: IaC Options
docs.text: Approach Module Dependencies Best For CDK agentkit-aws 21 AWS-native teams, CDK users Pulumi agentkit-pulumi-aws 340 Multi-cloud teams, Pulumi users CloudFormation agentkit only 0 (just yaml.v3) No IaC runtime, AWS CLI only

docs.title: CDK Deployment
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws/agentcore"

func main() {
    app := agentcore.NewApp()

    agentcore.NewStackBuilder("my-agents").
        WithAgents(research, orchestration).
        WithOpik("my-project", "arn:aws:secretsmanager:...").
        Build(app)

    agentcore.Synth(app)
}

cdk deploy

docs.title: Pulumi Deployment
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-pulumi-aws/agentcore"

func main() {
    pulumi.Run(func(ctx *pulumi.Context) error {
        _, err := agentcore.NewStackBuilder("my-agents").
            WithAgents(research, orchestration).
            WithOpik("my-project", "arn:aws:secretsmanager:...").
            Build(ctx)
        return err
    })
}

pulumi up

docs.title: Pure CloudFormation (No CDK/Pulumi)
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/agentkit/platforms/agentcore/iac"

func main() {
    config, _ := iac.LoadStackConfigFromFile("config.yaml")
    iac.GenerateCloudFormationFile(config, "template.yaml")
}

go run generate.go
aws cloudformation deploy \
    --template-file template.yaml \
    --stack-name my-agents \
    --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM

docs.title: EKS (Kubernetes)
docs.text: For EKS deployment, use standard Kubernetes tooling (Helm). agentkit provides Helm values structs in agentkit/platforms/kubernetes/.

docs.title: Why Helm Instead of CDK/Pulumi?
docs.text:
• Helm is the standard for Kubernetes app deployment
  • CDK/Pulumi for K8s apps adds unnecessary complexity
  • EKS cluster provisioning is typically done once by platform teams
  • Helm integrates with GitOps (ArgoCD, Flux)

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
# Using Helm
helm install my-agents ./charts/agentkit \
    -f values.yaml \
    --namespace agents

# Or kubectl with manifests
kubectl apply -f manifests/

docs.title: When to Use EKS vs AgentCore
docs.text: Choose EKS When Choose AgentCore When Need persistent connections Bursty, event-driven workloads Custom networking requirements Minimal ops desired Existing K8s infrastructure Pay-per-invocation preferred Need GPU access Cold start latency acceptable Long-running agents Short-lived agent tasks

docs.title: ECS/Fargate
docs.text: ECS with Fargate provides serverless containers without Kubernetes complexity. Support is planned but not yet implemented.

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
• Not yet implemented in agentkit-aws or agentkit-pulumi-aws
  • Can be added to agentkit-aws/ecs/ and agentkit-pulumi-aws/ecs/ if needed

docs.title: When to Consider ECS
docs.text:
• Want serverless without AgentCore preview limitations
  • Need more container configuration than AgentCore allows
  • Existing ECS infrastructure

docs.title: Module Architecture
docs.text:
agentkit/                              # Core library
├── platforms/
│   ├── agentcore/
│   │   ├── iac/                       # Shared IaC config
│   │   │   ├── config.go              # StackConfig, AgentConfig, etc.
│   │   │   ├── loader.go              # JSON/YAML loading
│   │   │   └── cloudformation.go      # Pure CF generator
│   │   └── *.go                       # AgentCore runtime
│   └── kubernetes/
│       └── values.go                  # Helm values structs

agentkit-aws/                          # AWS CDK (21 deps)
└── agentcore/                         # AgentCore via CDK

agentkit-pulumi-aws/                   # Pulumi AWS (340 deps)
└── agentcore/                         # AgentCore via Pulumi

docs.title: Dependency Strategy
docs.text:
Module Dependencies Rationale agentkit ~40 Core stays lean agentkit-aws +21 CDK uses jsii (lightweight) agentkit-pulumi-aws +340 Native Go Pulumi SDK

Separate modules ensure users only pull dependencies for their chosen IaC tool.

docs.title: Configuration Sharing
docs.text:
All IaC approaches share the same configuration schema:

# config.yaml - works with CDK, Pulumi, and CloudFormation
stackName: my-agents
description: My agent deployment

agents:
  - name: research
    containerImage: ghcr.io/example/research:latest
    memoryMB: 512
    timeoutSeconds: 30

  - name: orchestration
    containerImage: ghcr.io/example/orchestration:latest
    memoryMB: 1024
    timeoutSeconds: 300
    isDefault: true

vpc:
  createVPC: true
  vpcCidr: 10.0.0.0/16

observability:
  provider: opik
  project: my-agents
  enableCloudWatchLogs: true

tags:
  Environment: production

docs.title: Decision Tree
docs.text:
Need to deploy agentkit agents to AWS?
│
├─ Want serverless (no cluster management)?
│   │
│   ├─ OK with AgentCore preview? → Use AgentCore (CDK/Pulumi/CF)
│   │
│   └─ Need GA serverless? → Use ECS/Fargate (coming soon)
│
└─ Have/want Kubernetes?
    │
    └─ Use EKS + Helm

docs.title: AgentKit for AWS Pulumi v0.1.0 Release Notes
docs.text:
Release Date: December 31, 2025

Initial release of Pulumi components for deploying agentkit-based agents to AWS Bedrock AgentCore.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Native Go Pulumi components for AgentCore deployment
  • Fluent builder API matching agentkit-aws-cdk patterns
  • Shared configuration schema with agentkit core and agentkit-aws-cdk
  • Full AWS resource management via Pulumi state

docs.title: Pulumi Components
docs.text:
Native Go components for AWS Bedrock AgentCore:

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi/agentcore"
    "github.com/pulumi/pulumi/sdk/v3/go/pulumi"
)

func main() {
    pulumi.Run(func(ctx *pulumi.Context) error {
        _, err := agentcore.NewStackBuilder("my-agents").
            WithAgents(research, orchestration).
            WithOpik("my-project", secretARN).
            WithTags(map[string]string{"Environment": "production"}).
            Build(ctx)
        return err
    })
}

docs.title: Fluent Builders
docs.text:
• AgentBuilder - Configure individual agents with memory, timeout, environment variables
  • StackBuilder - Compose agents with VPC, observability, and IAM settings

docs.title: Configuration Loading
docs.text:
Load stack configuration from JSON or YAML files:

pulumi.Run(func(ctx *pulumi.Context) error {
    _, err := agentcore.NewStackFromFile(ctx, "config.yaml")
    return err
})

docs.title: AWS Resources Created
docs.text:
• VPC with public/private subnets across multiple AZs
  • NAT Gateway for private subnet internet access
  • Security Groups for agent network isolation
  • IAM Roles with Bedrock and Secrets Manager access
  • CloudWatch Log Groups with configurable retention

docs.title: Deployment Approaches
docs.text: Approach Description Go Constructs Full type safety, IDE support, complex logic JSON/YAML Config Configuration-driven with minimal Go wrapper

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Uses shared configuration schema from agentkit/platforms/agentcore/iac/:

stackName: my-agents
agents:
  - name: research
    containerImage: ghcr.io/example/research:latest
    memoryMB: 512
    timeoutSeconds: 30
  - name: orchestration
    containerImage: ghcr.io/example/orchestration:latest
    memoryMB: 1024
    isDefault: true
vpc:
  createVPC: true
  vpcCidr: 10.0.0.0/16
observability:
  provider: opik
  project: my-agents

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• 340 transitive packages (native Go Pulumi SDK)
  • Requires github.com/plexusone/agentkit for shared configuration
  • No Node.js runtime required (unlike CDK)

docs.title: Comparison with agentkit-aws-cdk
docs.text: Feature agentkit-aws-cdk agentkit-aws-pulumi Runtime Node.js (jsii) Native Go State management CloudFormation Pulumi Cloud/S3/Local Dependencies 21 340 Preview command cdk diff pulumi preview Deploy command cdk deploy pulumi up

docs.title: Related Modules
docs.text: Module Purpose agentkit Core library, shared IaC config, pure CloudFormation agentkit-aws-cdk AWS CDK constructs agentkit-terraform Terraform modules (planned)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Pulumi CLI: curl -fsSL https://get.pulumi.com | sh
  2. AWS credentials configured
  3. Pulumi project initialized: pulumi new go --name my-agents

docs.title: AgentKit for AWS Pulumi v0.2.0 Release Notes
docs.text:
Release Date: March 2, 2026

Organization migration release: module path and all dependencies migrated from github.com/agentplexus to github.com/plexusone.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Module path migration from github.com/agentplexus/agentkit-aws-pulumi to github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi
  • Shared CI/CD workflows migrated to plexusone/.github for standardized pipeline management

docs.title: Module Path Changed
docs.text:
The Go module path has changed:

- github.com/agentplexus/agentkit-aws-pulumi
+ github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi

docs.title: Upgrade Steps
docs.text:
1. Update your go.mod:
go get github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi@v0.2.0
  1. Update all import paths:
- "github.com/agentplexus/agentkit-aws-pulumi/agentcore"
+ "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi/agentcore"
  1. Run go mod tidy to clean up dependencies

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Updated dependency to github.com/plexusone/agentkit v0.5.0 (also migrated from agentplexus)
  • Docker image references updated from ghcr.io/agentplexus/ to ghcr.io/plexusone/

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text:
CI/CD workflows migrated to shared workflows from plexusone/.github:

  • go-ci.yaml - Build and test workflow
  • go-lint.yaml - golangci-lint workflow
  • go-sast-codeql.yaml - CodeQL security scanning

docs.title: AgentKit for AWS Pulumi v0.3.0 Release Notes
docs.text:
Release Date: April 20, 2026

This release adds the AWS Lightsail Container Service deployment provider, implementing the new deploy.Provider interface from agentkit v0.6.0.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• AWS Lightsail deployment provider for containerized agent deployments
  • Pulumi automation utilities for programmatic stack management

docs.title: Lightsail Deployment Provider
docs.text:
Deploy agents to AWS Lightsail Container Service with a simple import:

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/agentkit/deploy"
    _ "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi/deploy/providers/lightsail"
)

cfg, _ := deploy.LoadDeployConfig("deploy.yaml")
provider, _ := deploy.GetProvider(cfg)
defer provider.Close()

status, _ := provider.Deploy(ctx, cfg)
fmt.Println(status.Outputs["serviceUrl"])

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configure deployments via YAML:

stack:
  name: my-agent-prod
  project: my-project
  region: us-east-1
  image:
    repository: myorg/myagent
    tag: v1.0.0
  resources:
    port: 8080
  health_check:
    path: /health

provider: lightsail

docs.title: Lightsail Power Levels
docs.text: Power vCPU Memory nano 0.25 512 MB micro 0.5 1 GB small 1 2 GB medium 2 4 GB large 4 8 GB xlarge 8 16 GB

docs.title: Pulumi Utilities
docs.text:
The deploy/pulumi package provides automation utilities:

import "github.com/plexusone/agentkit-aws-pulumi/deploy/pulumi"

stack, err := pulumi.NewStack(ctx, pulumi.StackOptions{
    ProjectName: "my-project",
    StackName:   "prod",
    BackendURL:  "s3://my-bucket/pulumi",
    Config: map[string]string{
        "aws:region": "us-east-1",
    },
}, program)

result, err := stack.Up(ctx)
fmt.Printf("Created: %d, Updated: %d\n", result.Summary.Create, result.Summary.Update)

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• deploy/pulumi/stack.go - Pulumi automation utilities (Stack, StackOptions, UpResult)
  • deploy/providers/lightsail/lightsail.go - Lightsail Provider implementation
  • deploy/providers/lightsail/config.go - Lightsail-specific configuration
  • deploy/providers/lightsail/init.go - Auto-registration via init()
